title: Protocol: Africa and the Global South comparison of interventional clinical trial density
body: This cross-sectional registry study will compare interventional trial density across Africa, Latin America, and South and Southeast Asia using ClinicalTrials.gov as the sole data source. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for interventional trial counts across 25 countries using filter.advanced with AREA[StudyType]INTERVENTIONAL and query.locn for location, calculating trials per million population. The primary analysis will compare regional population-weighted averages, quantify the Africa Penalty defined as developing-world average divided by Africa average, and include a pairwise comparison of Nigeria versus India as economically matched nations with divergent trial portfolios. Secondary analyses will examine condition-specific portfolio comparison for HIV, cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, phase distribution by region, World Bank income-group stratification, and Spearman rank correlation of GDP per capita against trial density. Query logic, country lists, and population denominators will be version controlled for reproducibility. No patient-level data will be used. Limitations include dependence on a single registry, reliance on approximate population estimates, and ecological study design.

sentences.text: This cross-sectional registry study will compare interventional trial density across Africa, Latin America, and South and Southeast Asia.
We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for interventional trial counts across 25 countries and calculate trials per million population for each.
The primary analysis will compare regional weighted averages and quantify the gap between Africa and other lower- and middle-income regions.
Secondary analyses will examine matched country comparisons, condition portfolios, and the association between income level and trial density.
Query logic, country lists, and denominators will be version controlled for reproducibility.
No patient-level data will be used.
Limitations include dependence on a single registry and on approximate population estimates.

ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.name: methods before outcomes
checks.detail: API query method in sentence 2 precedes primary analysis definition in sentence 4.

checks.name: limitations stated
checks.detail: Final sentence addresses single-registry limitation and population estimate reliance.
